title: Procedure for processing of applications under Section 11A of the Prevention of Money-Laundering Act, 2002 for use of Aadhaar authentication services by entities other than Banking companies.
description: Procedure for processing of applications under Section 11A of the Prevention of Money-Laundering Act, 2002 for use of Aadhaar authentication services by entities other than Banking companies.
text: F. NO.P-120111712019-ES fell-DoR Government of Ind a Ministry of Financ Department of Reve ue CIRCULAR New Delhi, 9th May, 2019 Subject: Procedure for processing of applic,tions under Section 11A of the Prevention of Money-Laundering tct, 2002 for use of Aadhaar authentication services by entities other than Banking companies. 1. Section 11A of the Prevention of Money aundering Act, 2002 (hereinafter referred to as the Act) and the rules made ther under provide that any reporting entity being a banking company can accept Aadh ar number from its client either at their option or when the client intends to reCeiVejny benefit or subsidy under any scheme notified under section 7 of the Aadhaar ( argeted Delivery of Financial and Other subsidies, Benefits and Services) Act, 2016 nd carry out authentication of the clie1t's Aadhaar number using e-KYC auth~nticati[n facility pr~,vided by the Unique Identification Authority of India. 1 , " 2. The Act also provides that the Central GOI ernment may, if satisfied that a reporting entity other than banking company, complies with such standards of privacy and security under the Aadhaar (Targete Delivery of Financial and Other Subsidies, Benefits and Services) Act, 2016, and i is necessary and expedient to do so, by notification, permit such entity to carry ut authentication of the client's Aadhaar number using e-KYC authentication facil ty. However, no such notification shall be issued without consultation with the Uniq e Identification Authority of India (UIDAI) established under sub-section (1) of section 11 of the Aadhaar (Targeted Delivery of Financial and Other Subsidies, Benefit~ and Services) Act, 2016 and the appropriate regulator. 3. Any reporting entity which desires to car out authentication of the client's Aadhaar number using e-KYC authentication facilit needs to be notified under these provisions and before any such notification is iss ed, the appropriate regulator and UIDAI should be satisfied of the credentials of s ch reporting entity. The following procedure is being laid down for entities which d sire to carry out authentication of the client's Aadhaar number using e-KYC authenti ation facility: Step 1: Application All applications by concerned entities unde Section 11A of the Act for use of Aadhaar authentication services shall be filed bef re the appropriate regulator. The -- 1 of 4 -- • I •• -2- regulator will be at liberty in which information from the applicant is to be obtained. Step 2: Examination by the appropriate gulator (a) The Regulator shall examine the pplications filed before it, upon submission of the prescribed fees, if any, to ensure that the requisite conditions under Section 11A of the Act and any other requirements laid down by the regulator and tho e under the law are met by the applicant. Upon scrutiny, if the proposed purpose for Aadhaar authentication is found to be necessary and expedie·1 t by the appropriate regulator, it shall forward such application to UID I along with its recommendation. (b) (c) Upon scrutiny, if it is found that the a plicant fails to fulfill the requisite conditions, it shall reject such applica ions. UIDAI shall scrutinize the apPlicati{S received by it from various regulators, upon submission of the prescribed fees, if any. Each application shall be examined i~ light \ f the recommendations received: from the appropriate regulator. UIDAr will be at liberty to specify any format in which additional information from the applicant is to be obtained for its examination for gran~ of Authentication User Agency (AUA)/ E-KYC User Agency (KUA) liI:nse under the Aadhaar Act and regulation framed thereunder. The examination shall be conducted n the basis of such standards of privacy and security as may be spe ified by UIDAI from time to time under the Aadhaar Act and Regulatio s framed thereunder. Step 3: Examination by UIDAI (a) (b) (c) After examining, UIDAI shall send its recommendation along with any conditions, if any, to Department of Revenue for notification under Section 11A of the PMLA Act. Step 4: Notification by the Central Govern ent The Central Government, if satisfied ith the recommendations of the regulator and UIDAI that the applicant fulfills all conditions under section 11A, may by notification permit such applicant 0 perform authentication under clause (a) of section 11A. Step 5: Based on permission given by the Central Government, UIDAI will issue necessary authorization to the applicant for conducting authentication -- 2 of 4 -- -3- which will be subject to payment of fees nd compliance of the terms and conditions of KUAlAUA license under the A dhaar Act and regulations framed thereunder. 4. The regulators are requested to monitor compliance with respect to the requirements laid down for performing authentic tion under clause (a) of section 11A, by all reporting entities with respect to whic a notification to this effect has been issued, as part of their regulatory supervisio . On observance of any default, the regulators may take necessary action und r their laws, inter-alia including suspension and cancellation, and also bring it to the notice of Department of Revenue for further necessary action. 5. Similarly, UIDAI is also requested to put in p ace systems to ensure continuing compliance by the reporting entities which have b en notified under section 11A of the Act to carry out authentication of the client' Aadhaar number using e-KYC authentication facility to the standards of privacy nd security prescribed by UIDAI. Where UIDAI notices any default by the reportin entities, it may take necessary action under the Aadhaar Act and regulations fram d thereunder, inter-alia including suspension and cancellation and also bring it 0 the notice of Department of Revenue for further necessary action. ! I 6. I At any point, after issue of such n6tific tion, based On a report of the " appropriate regulator or UIDAI or otherwise, if it is found that the reporting entity no longer fulfils the requirements for performing au hentication under clause (a) of section 11A, the Central Government may withdr: w the notification after giving an opportunity to the reporting entity.
